message:
test(conformance): wire munmap/mprotect/madvise to differential execution against host libc

Three more conformance harness stubs converted from "stubbed return
'0'" to real differential execution against host libc, mirroring
the kill() rewrite.

Previous shape (all three identical):
  fn execute_X_case(mode) {
      ensure_supported_mode(mode)?;
      // Would do X — needs valid mapped addr, stub
      Ok(non_host_execution("0".to_string()))
  }

That gave us nothing — every X call returned "0" regardless of
inputs, so the harness couldn't catch a regression where
frankenlibc_abi::mmap_abi::munmap accidentally returned -1, or
where mprotect silently succeeded on PROT_NONE pages it shouldn't
have, etc.

New shape: each case parses the relevant inputs (length, plus prot
or advice for the protection / madvise cases), allocates a fresh
private-anonymous mapping via the impl, runs the operation under
test, and in strict mode also runs the same operation on a
parallel mapping created via the host libc, then returns
parity_execution.

Two new helpers — map_impl_private_anonymous and
map_host_private_anonymous — encapsulate the mmap call so the
three executors don't repeat the MAP_PRIVATE | MAP_ANONYMOUS
pattern, and so the error messages distinguish "FrankenLibC
anonymous mapping of N bytes" from "host anonymous mapping of N
bytes" to make a setup failure debuggable from the report alone.

execute_munmap_case
- inputs: { length: usize }. Parsed via parse_usize_any so fixtures
  can use either "length" or "len" as the key.
- Allocate via map_impl_private_anonymous(length, RW), call
  munmap on the impl pointer, capture impl_rc.
- Strict: allocate via map_host_private_anonymous(length, RW),
  call host libc::munmap, return parity_execution.

execute_mprotect_case
- inputs: { length: usize, prot: i32 }.
- Allocate RW, call mprotect(impl_ptr, length, prot), then munmap
  the impl mapping so the test doesn't leak it (parity comparison
  is on the mprotect rc only). Strict mirrors with host.

execute_madvise_case (same pattern; not shown above but follows
the same shape with an advice: i32 input).

Update the dispatch table in execute_fixture_case so the three
match arms now pass `inputs` through (previously they dropped
inputs since the bodies didn't use them).

Existing strict-mode parity_execution machinery handles the host
result.to_string() vs impl rc comparison; no changes there. Mode
support stays exactly as ensure_supported_mode allows — no new
modes introduced.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
